message:
Quick question: could you pick your dog out of a lineup… by their nose? 🐶👃 Turns out, nature already can.

Just like every person has a one-of-a-kind set of fingerprints, every dog has a completely unique nose print. The tiny ridges, bumps and grooves on that cool, wet snout form a pattern that no other dog on Earth shares — not even littermates, not even two pups that look identical.

And here's the part that surprises most people: it's not some brand-new discovery. The Canadian Kennel Club has accepted dog nose prints as official proof of identity since 1938. Long before microchips existed, a simple inked nose print could prove exactly which dog was which.

That same idea is making a comeback today. There are now phone apps that snap a photo of a dog's nose and use the pattern to help reunite lost pups with their families — a built-in ID badge your dog has carried since puppyhood.

So the next time you get a cold little nose boop, remember: you're being booped by the most exclusive signature in the world. 🐾💛

Give your pup a nose boop from us and tell us their name below! 👇
